storage_extensions.usage: mmap codes.bin; at IS HT build time, for each qualifying IS+fy2023 pre row i: plabel_code = plabel_codes[i]. Store plabel_code in IS HT value. Use plabel_code as integer group-by key component. At output decode: dict_offsets[plabel_code]..dict_offsets[plabel_code+1] gives byte span in dict_data.

join_order.notes: Flat bool array fy2023[86135] indexed by adsh_code; 84KB, L1-resident.

join_order.build: pre(stmt=IS, adsh in fy2023) → flat open-addressing hash table keyed by packed(adsh,tag,ver)
join_order.probe: num rows passing fy2023 filter (~6.5M rows)
join_order.notes: KEY CHANGE from iter_1: apply fy2023 filter during IS HT build. Only IS pre rows where fy2023[pre_adsh_col[i]]==1 are inserted (~325K rows instead of 1.73M). IS HT: IS_HT_SIZE=2^20=1048576 slots x 16 bytes = 16MB. Load factor = 325K/1048576 = 31%. Average successful probe = 1.5 (vs 3.28 at 82%). 16MB fits comfortably in L3 alongside per-thread TL maps, reducing probe cost from DRAM (~50ns) to L3 (~12ns) during parallel scan.

filters.notes: Applied during IS HT build to eliminate 81% of IS rows that will never be probed

scan.access_pattern: sequential with num_zonemaps block skipping; software prefetch for IS HT probe slot

data_structures.is_pre_hashtable: flat open-addressing HT; IS_HT_SIZE=2^20=1048576 slots x 16 bytes = 16MB; key=uint64_t packed(adsh:17b|tag:18b|ver:17b); value=uint64_t packed(mult:16b|row_id:24b|plabel_code:20b); built from ~325K fy2023+IS pre rows; 31% load; L3-resident during scan. CHANGE: IS_HT_SIZE halved from 2^21 to 2^20 due to fy2023 filter reducing entries 5.3x.
data_structures.tl_raw_alloc: SINGLE contiguous allocation: new AggSlot[(size_t)nthreads * TL_SIZE]; TL_SIZE=2^18=262144; each thread uses tl_raw + (size_t)tid*TL_SIZE as its private 6.3MB TL map. CRITICAL: initialized in parallel (each thread fills its own slice) to eliminate ~163ms of single-threaded page faults.
data_structures.tl_compact: per-thread std::vector<AggEntry> allocated and filled inside parallel region; ~41K entries per thread; 24 bytes each
data_structures.parallel_merge_sub_hts: per-thread sub-HT for parallel merge: SUB_HT_SIZE=2^11=2048 slots x 24 bytes = 48KB per thread; L2-resident; thread t owns entries where hash64(key) % nthreads == t
data_structures.plabel_codes: mmap column_versions/pre.plabel.dict/codes.bin; uint32_t[9600799]; read sequentially during IS HT build

parallelism.threads: nthreads = omp_get_max_threads(); cap at 64 if hyperthreading doubles count
parallelism.notes: Three parallel regions: (1) parallel_preinit — each thread initializes tl_raw slice (6.3MB/thread) and IS HT slice (16MB/nthreads) to avoid single-threaded page fault bottleneck; (2) parallel_scan_probe_aggregate — morsel-driven USD block scan with IS HT probe + aggregation into thread-local maps; (3) parallel_merge_aggregate — partitioned merge where thread t handles hash(key) % nthreads == t, inserts into L2-resident 48KB sub-HT, no contention.

indexes.purpose: skip_blocks — identify USD-containing blocks in num; distribute block morsels to threads
skip_blocks — during IS HT build, skip non-IS stmt blocks (IS rows contiguous since pre sorted by stmt,adsh)

output.sort_strategy: std::partial_sort over all groups (top 200); collect from all nthreads sub-HTs

optimization_notes: Three root causes identified from execution_results.json: (1) 256ms untracked within the total phase timer (named phases only sum to 194ms), dominated by single-threaded initialization of 64x6.3MB=403MB thread-local aggregation maps BEFORE the parallel_scan phase timer starts — each std::vector fill triggers ~100K page faults on the main thread (~163ms alone). Fix: Category B — allocate tl_maps as ONE contiguous array and initialize in a parallel pre-init OMP region so each thread faults and fills only its own 6.3MB slice (~2ms wall vs ~163ms serial). Same treatment for is_ht and gm. (2) build_is_pre_hashtable (79ms): currently inserts ALL 1.73M IS pre rows, but only ~325K have adsh codes in the fy2023 set — the other 81% will NEVER be probed since num rows are already filtered by fy2023 before probing the IS HT. Fix: Category C — add fy2023[pre_adsh_col[i]] check during IS HT build. IS HT shrinks to 2^20=16MB at 31% load (avg 1.5 probes), fits cleanly in L3, and build is 5.3x faster with lower probe lengths. (3) parallel_scan_probe_aggregate (81ms): 64 threads accessing 32MB IS HT + individual TL maps creates total working set 160MB >> 44MB L3, pushing IS HT probes to DRAM latency (~50ns per probe). Smaller IS HT (16MB, fy2023-filtered) fits in L3 alongside thread working sets, cutting probe cost to L3 latency (~12ns). Additionally, merge_aggregate (25ms) is a serial 2.6M-entry merge — replaced with a partitioned parallel merge where thread t scans all compact lists keeping only hash(key)%nthreads==t entries and inserts into its own L2-resident 48KB sub-HT (zero contention, ~8ms wall). Combined projected savings: ~305ms reduction, from 472ms to ~167ms, beating the 171ms target.

correctness_anchors.usd_code: loaded at runtime from shared/uom.dict (pre-seeded index 0)
correctness_anchors.is_code: loaded at runtime from shared/stmt.dict (pre-seeded index 1)
